name: Sycamore
description: A reactive library for creating web apps in Rust and WebAssembly with fine-grained reactivity

description.value: A reactive library for creating web apps in Rust and WebAssembly with fine-grained reactivity

active-maintenance.comment: Active development with 646 commits, 72 contributors, 28 releases; used by 820 dependent projects

reactivity-model.comment: Fine-grained reactivity with signals; only parts of the app that need to be updated will be, without Virtual DOM

component-model.comment: Components defined as Rust functions with #[component] attribute macro

rendering-paradigm.comment: Declarative view! macro syntax with fine-grained reactive updates

state-management-approach.comment: Built-in reactive primitives with create_signal() for state management

opinionatedness.comment: Provides core primitives but allows flexibility in architecture; can use DSL or builder API

backward-compatibility.comment: Still pre-1.0 (v0.9.x); APIs evolving with Reactivity v3 introduced in v0.9.0

compiles-to.comment: Compiles Rust to WebAssembly for the browser; also supports server-side rendering

typescript-support.comment: Rust framework; not applicable - uses Rust's type system instead

template-syntax.comment: view! macro with custom DSL; also supports builder API for type-checked UI

styling-approach.comment: No built-in styling; works with external CSS and Tailwind

build-required.comment: Requires Rust toolchain with wasm32-unknown-unknown target; Trunk is the recommended build tool

wasm-interop.comment: Uses wasm-bindgen for JavaScript interop; wasm-bindgen-interning feature optimizes performance

computed-values.comment: Derived/computed values that auto-update via reactive system

two-way-binding.comment: Supports two-way binding for form elements via bind directive

event-handling.comment: Declarative event binding in view! macro

conditional-rendering.comment: Conditional rendering supported in view! macro

list-rendering.comment: Keyed and indexed list rendering supported

teleport-portal.comment: No documented portal/teleport component in current version

suspense.comment: Suspense API with render_to_string_await_suspense() for async loading boundaries

context-provide-inject.comment: Context API for dependency injection without prop drilling

refs-direct-dom-access.comment: Direct DOM manipulation available via wasm-bindgen and web-sys

official-router.comment: sycamore-router is the official first-party routing solution

nested-routes.comment: Not explicitly documented in router API; may be supported via route enum patterns

dynamic-routes.comment: Parameterized routes via TryFromParam and TryFromSegments traits for typed route parameters

route-guards.comment: Not explicitly documented in router API

lazy-loading-routes.comment: Not explicitly documented in router API

scroll-restoration.comment: Not explicitly documented in router API

built-in-global-state.comment: Context API supports app-wide state management

global-state-library.comment: Built-in context and signals sufficient; no separate global state library

devtools-state-inspection.comment: No official browser devtools extension; debugging via Rust tooling

official-cli.comment: Uses Trunk as build tool; no dedicated Sycamore CLI for project scaffolding

hot-module-replacement.comment: Trunk provides hot reloading for development

browser-devtools.comment: No official browser devtools extension; uses standard browser WASM debugging

vs-code-extension.comment: rust-analyzer provides Rust language support; no Sycamore-specific extension

error-messages.comment: Rust's excellent compiler errors; type-checked UI catches errors at compile-time

documentation-quality.comment: 100% documented API via docs.rs; Sycamore Book available; adequate but less extensive than larger frameworks

interactive-tutorial.comment: Sycamore Book guides through basic concepts to advanced topics

playground.comment: No official online playground due to Rust compilation requirements

bundle-size-core.comment: ~150 kB WASM gzipped for minimal app with release optimizations; varies by features. WASM sizes not directly comparable to JS bundles.

bundle-size-full.comment: ~250 kB WASM gzipped with router; can be optimized with opt-level='s', lto=true, panic='abort'. Not directly comparable to JS bundle sizes.

virtual-dom.comment: No Virtual DOM; fine-grained reactivity updates specific DOM nodes directly

compile-time-optimization.comment: Rust compiler performs extensive optimizations; view! macro generates efficient code at compile time; type-checks UI

tree-shaking.comment: Rust's dead code elimination; wasm-opt further optimizes WASM binaries

code-splitting.comment: No built-in WASM binary splitting; compiles to single WASM binary

ssr-capable.comment: Full SSR support with render_to_string() and hydration

partial-hydration.comment: Full hydration; no islands architecture support

js-framework-benchmark.comment: Estimated ~1.15 geometric mean; performs behind Leptos/Dioxus but ahead of React/Vue. Official benchmark example exists in repo.

npm-weekly-downloads.comment: Rust crate, not npm package; distributed via crates.io. ~829 weekly downloads on crates.io

meta-framework.comment: Perseus is the meta-framework for Sycamore providing SSR/SSG and file-based routing

major-companies-using.comment: No publicly documented major company adoption

official-sponsor-backer.comment: Community-driven project; no major corporate backing

discord-community-size.comment: ~623 members in Sycamore Discord as of December 2025

stack-overflow-questions.comment: Very limited Stack Overflow presence; community primarily uses Discord

job-market-demand.comment: Niche; smaller community than Leptos; primarily adopted by Rust enthusiasts

official-testing-library.comment: No official testing library; uses wasm-bindgen-test and expect-test as dev dependencies

testing-library-name.comment: Uses wasm-bindgen-test for WASM testing; expect-test for snapshot testing

component-testing.comment: Via wasm-bindgen-test; component tests in examples directory

e2e-testing-integration.comment: Compatible with Playwright and other E2E testing tools

pwa-support.comment: Can be configured as PWA via Trunk; no built-in tooling

desktop-framework.comment: Compatible with Tauri for desktop applications

learning-curve.comment: Steep learning curve; requires Rust knowledge plus framework concepts (signals, macros, reactivity)

concepts-to-learn.comment: Rust ownership, signals, view! macro, context, routing, SSR, suspense, component props

time-to-hello-world.comment: ~10 minutes with Rust toolchain installed; requires Trunk installation

migration-path.comment: No migration guides from JS frameworks; fundamentally different paradigm

incremental-adoption.comment: Cannot be incrementally added to existing JS applications; requires full Rust toolchain
